features.description: Core infrastructure: config, LLM client + cache, GSM8K dataset loader, answer parser, smoke tests
steps: set up project layout per spec §3.1 (agentdiet/ package)
implement config.py with model, paths, seeds
implement llm_client.py with OpenAI-compat client and sha256(model||temp||messages) cache (JSONL, atomic append, startup validation)
implement dataset.py to load GSM8K and sample n questions with fixed seed
implement answer parser handling '#### 42', '42.0', '$42' variants
write tests/test_smoke.py (Layer 1): cache hit, dataset non-empty, answer parser variants

features.description: Multi-agent debate runner: Agent class + scheduler for N agents × R rounds
steps: implement agents.py with Agent class (id, system_prompt, conversation_history) and 3 differentiated system prompts (solver/skeptic/synthesizer)
implement debate.py with run_debate(question, n_agents=3, n_rounds=3) producing a Dialogue with all messages + meta
implement majority-vote final_answer aggregation over last round
add Dialogue invariant assertion at end of run (message count == n_agents × n_rounds)
Layer 1 smoke test: minimal 2×1 debate produces schema-valid Dialogue

features.description: Pilot experiment: 30-question single-agent vs 3×3 debate comparison (Gate 1)
steps: implement baseline single-agent runner (round 1, agent 1 only)
run both on same 30-question sample (seed fixed)
report acc_single, acc_debate, delta, and 3 sample dialogues for sanity inspection
record pilot output to artifacts/pilot_report.md
GATE 1 decision: if delta < 3pp, stop and switch model (Qwen → GPT-4o-mini) before continuing

features.description: Full dialogue collection: 100 GSM8K questions × 3×3 debate, artifact per question
steps: implement cli/collect.py with --n, --resume, --model flags
write dialogues to artifacts/dialogues/{model}/{qid}.json (atomic)
log failures to failures/debate/{qid}.json with raw context + traceback
end-of-run invariant: n_collected == n_requested - n_failed
run full 100-question collection, verify at least 95 succeed

features.description: Schema-guided claim extraction with 6-type taxonomy + 10-dialogue spot check
steps: define Claim pydantic model per spec §3.3
write extraction prompt with 6-type taxonomy definitions + 3 few-shot examples
implement extract_claims.py using vLLM structured output (JSON mode)
one retry with stricter prompt on schema failure; log raw output to failures/ on final failure
run on all 100 dialogues, write artifacts/claims/{model}/{qid}.json
invariant: source_message_span within referenced message, type in enum
manual spot check on 10 random dialogues, record to artifacts/spot_check.csv with pass/fail notes

features.description: Flip-point localization + independent per-claim signals (4 fields, no composite)
steps: implement analysis/flip.py: detect rounds where agent answer majority flipped from wrong to right; output FlipEvent per flip with triggering_claim_id, pre/post answers

implement analysis/signals.py: for each claim compute flip_coincidence (bool), novelty (1 - max cos sim via sentence-transformer), referenced_later (bool via 0.7 cos sim threshold), position (round index)
output flip_events.jsonl + signal_scores.parquet
IMPORTANT: do NOT construct a composite score — store 4 independent fields only (spec §5.2)
invariant: every triggering_claim_id exists in claims artifact

features.description: Type-level ablation: causal importance Delta_t per claim type on single_wrong∧debate_right subset (Gate 2)
steps: sample ~20 dialogues from single_wrong ∧ debate_right subset
for each of 6 types t: reconstruct history with type-t claims removed, replay final round via llm_client (cache-keyed on new context)
compute Delta_t = acc(with) - acc(without) per type
enforce MAX_NEW_LLM_CALLS=500 hard cap
output artifacts/analysis/ablation.jsonl with per-type Delta, sample sizes, skipped cases
GATE 2 decision: if all Delta_t within noise, accept null result and adjust Day 3 compression scope (descriptive comparison rather than data-supported rule)

features.description: Data-driven compression policy derived from Day 2 findings + compress.apply implementation
steps: human reads ablation.jsonl + signal_scores + flip_events; writes artifacts/compression/policy.json with simplest data-supported rule (spec §5.3)
implement compress.py with apply(dialogue, policy) producing compressed history string
support policy shapes: drop_types (list of types), drop_low_novelty (threshold), drop_unreferenced (bool), or logistic-regression-derived weighted rule if single signal insufficient
same interface also implements b1 (no-op), b2 (round1 agent1 only), b3 (last-k-rounds), b5 (random drop with seed)

features.description: Evaluation sweep: 5 methods × 100 questions × (accuracy, tokens, acc_per_1k)
steps: implement evaluate.py: for each method in {b1, b2, b3, b5, ours}, replay final round using compressed history; compute accuracy (vs gold), total input tokens, accuracy-per-1k-tokens
output artifacts/evaluation/results.json
sanity invariants: acc(b1) ≥ acc(b2); acc(ours) ≥ acc(b5); token ordering b1 > b3 > b5 ≈ ours
also log per-question breakdown for error analysis

features.description: Analysis figures, tables, and IMRAD report PDF (d3 + d4)
steps: analysis notebook producing: claim type distribution (by agent × round), signal-vs-flip correlations (separately), Delta_t ranking bar chart, Pareto curve (acc vs tokens across methods)
LaTeX tables for baseline comparison and claim statistics

write IMRAD report ~6-8 pages: abstract, intro (pivot rationale), methods (3×3 debate on GSM8K, claim extraction, flip+ablation attribution), results, discussion (including null-result framing if Gate 2 triggers), limitations (single-seed, n=100, no multi-seed bootstrap)
export PDF to docs/reports/final-report.pdf

features.description: Dedupe _format_other_responses: debate.py and analysis/ablate.py have near-identical copies (sp-feedback f-20260422-02)
steps: export _format_other_responses from agentdiet/debate.py
update agentdiet/analysis/ablate.py to import the shared helper
remove duplicate local definition in ablate.py
verify full test suite still passes with no regressions

features.description: Qwen learned prompt literal 'N' and outputs '#### N66'; parser regex fails, falls back to first $<num> in text (often a wrong price), polluting pilot/collection final_answer fields
steps: loosen #### regex in agentdiet/dataset.py to allow optional [A-Za-z]* between #### and number
change agents.py prompt from '#### N' literal to '#### <answer>' with example
add tests locking the new regex + agent-prompt invariants
add cli/reparse.py that reads existing pilot + dialogue artifacts and recomputes final_answer + per_agent_final_answers in place (no LLM calls)
update README HPC runbook with 'after pulling this fix, run python -m agentdiet.cli.reparse' step

features.description: 230/900 (25.6%) claim-extraction messages failed — root cause: LLM puts LaTeX '\(' and '\[' in quote strings; JSON spec rejects \( as invalid escape so json.loads fails. Pre-process raw response to fix-up non-JSON escapes before parsing.
steps: add _fix_json_escapes helper in extract_claims.py: regex-sub '\\' followed by non-JSON-escape char -> '\\\\' + that char
wire helper into _parse_claims_payload BEFORE json.loads
add tests with real failure samples (LaTeX \\( \\[ \\frac etc)
verify: rate-limit test — no regressions on existing valid JSON without backslashes

features.description: Ablate replay Δ=0 for all 6 types because span-level masking only covers 26% of message text on average (median 20%, 27% of messages <10% coverage). LLM re-solves GSM8K from remaining reasoning. Add message-level ablation: if a message contains any drop_type claim, blank the whole message.
steps: add granularity='message' branch to reconstruct_masked_history in analysis/ablate.py
update replay_final_round + run_ablation to accept granularity kwarg
cli/ablate.py: add --granularity flag defaulting to 'message'
tests: message-level removes whole messages, span-level unchanged
README note: ablate is message-level; compress.apply stays span-level (different goals)

features.description: Control experiment for Gate-2 null-result: blank ALL messages in history (keep only question), same 9 subset, measure acc_without. Distinguishes (a) no type matters from (b) single-type ablation too weak. Prerequisite to deciding if descriptive attribution is needed.
steps: add --control flag to cli/ablate.py that blanks every message round 1..final-1
compute single control row per qid, output control_result.json
README note on how to interpret control result vs gate2_report

features.description: L1 cross-model replication: drop-all control on Llama-3.1-8B doesn't need claim extraction, only pilot single + debate dialogues. Add require_claims=False mode to select_subset and wire --control to use it. Saves ~30-40 min per replication model.
steps: add require_claims kwarg to select_subset (default True for backwards compat)
wire --control branch in cli/ablate.py to call select_subset with require_claims=False
tests covering both modes
README note that --control no longer needs make extract first

features.description: evalscope integration for code datasets and sandbox; multi-year AIME loader; code-domain debate protocol with proposer/reviewer/integrator roles
steps: Install evalscope; wrap dataset loaders for HumanEval+ and LiveCodeBench (filter contest_date>=2024-08) behind Dataset interface
Wrap evalscope code-execution sandbox behind Judge interface; verify pass@1 on a known-correct sample
Implement multi-year AIME loader (2026 full + 2025 full + 2024 random 20 with seed 42) producing 80-question pool
Implement code-domain debate protocol in agentdiet/debate/code_protocol.py with proposer/reviewer/integrator roles and Notes/Code message schema
Implement functional clustering for both voting baseline and debate aggregation using public test signatures
Add unit tests for schema parsing, 3-way clustering, and AIME year stratification (30/30/20)

features.description: 16-cell main grid: 2 models x 4 datasets x thinking on/off, with SA / voting / debate per cell; pilot first to catch protocol bugs
steps: Run math pilot: Qwen3-30B-A3B + GSM8K + thinking off, all 3 conditions; verify pipeline produces well-defined N for voting calibration
Run code pilot: Qwen3-30B-A3B + HumanEval+ + thinking off, all 3 conditions; iterate code protocol on bugs before scaling
Execute main grid thinking-off cells (8 cells): both models x 4 datasets x SA/voting/debate; output per-cell summary.json
Execute main grid thinking-on cells (8 cells): same structure with thinking enabled
Save artifacts to artifacts/grid/{model}__{dataset}__t{0,1}/ with sa.json, voting.json, debate.json, summary.json

features.description: Test whether collapse-to-voting depends on prompt design via 3 role-prompt variants on Qwen3-30B-A3B + GSM8K x thinking on/off
steps: Implement adversarial-strict prompt variant in agentdiet/prompts.py (skeptic must produce >=1 concrete disagreement)
Implement symmetric prompt variant (3 identical agents, no role differentiation)
Run Qwen3-30B-A3B + GSM8K x 3 prompt variants x thinking on/off (6 cells x 3 conditions)
Compare Delta(debate-voting) across prompt variants; flag any variant where Delta becomes statistically distinguishable from 0

features.description: Per-cell Delta with bootstrap CIs; phase diagram and per-axis characterizations; final IMRAD report with phase diagram as main figure
steps: Compute Delta = acc(debate) - acc(voting) per cell with paired bootstrap (10000 resamples) for 95% CI
Generate phase diagram scatter (X: SA accuracy, Y: Delta, color: thinking, shape: domain, fill: over_budget) saved to docs/reports/figs/phase_diagram.pdf

Per-axis characterization: per-dataset stratification by reasoning steps; AIME per-year analysis (contamination check); cross-model agreement table; round-by-round trajectory; voting-wrong-debate-right reverse-attribution case study
Compute thinking-axis observations O1 (Delta on vs off per cell) and O2 (acc(debate, off) vs acc(SA, on) per cell)
Assemble IMRAD LaTeX report with phase diagram as main figure; cite Du 2023 and Wang 2023; methodological appendix repurposes claim-extraction findings as mechanism explanation
Generate PDF via pandoc/LaTeX

features.description: Local part of cross-model-grid: extend llm_client with thinking:bool param + per-(model, thinking, top_p) cache partition + ChatResult with usage; implement agentdiet/voting.py (calibrate_n + run_voting + over_budget logging) per spec §5.3; integration tests. No GPU needed.
steps: Extend agentdiet/llm_client.py with thinking:bool parameter, ChatResult dataclass, OpenAIBackend model_family literal, per-(model, thinking, top_p) cache key

Add agentdiet/voting.py: calibrate_n(debate_tokens, sa_tokens) -> CalibrationResult with N>=3 floor + over_budget_factor; run_voting(question, n_samples, llm_client, ...) -> VotingResult using nonce-prefix sample-id trick
Integration tests covering toggle round-trip, calibration determinism, voting + calibrate composition

features.description: HPC GPU stand-up of Qwen3-30B-A3B + GPT-OSS-20B vLLM endpoints with smoke-tests verifying enable_thinking and reasoning_effort toggles work end-to-end. Requires user-side GPU time.
steps: Author scripts/serving/qwen3_a3b_serve.sh + smoke_qwen3.py + runbook; user runs on HPC, commits artifacts/serving/qwen3_smoke.json (5 calls each toggle, observed token deltas)
Author scripts/serving/gpt_oss_20b_serve.sh + smoke_gpt_oss.py + runbook; user runs on HPC, commits artifacts/serving/gpt_oss_smoke.json (reasoning_effort low vs high token deltas)
Verify both smoke artifacts pass schema check (test runner asserts presence + thinking-on > thinking-off output token mean)
